name: Komi-Permyak
notes: Komi-Permyak (перем коми / коми-пермяцкӧй кыв) of Perm Krai, written in a Cyrillic alphabet extended with ⟨ӧ⟩ and ⟨і⟩. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Komi orthography encodes consonant palatalisation through the following vowel letter and the soft sign, in a system that differs from Russian; a reliable letter → IPA table requires a descriptive source that has not been consulted. The historic Molodtsov alphabet is not covered. Metadata only.
